moveAnnotations.annotation: We're playing the Polish Opening, an offbeat choice that immediately challenges Black's spatial control of the center with a flank move. This move prepares to support a future e4 advance and puts our bishop on the long diagonal (after Bb2), but it's unusual enough that most opponents won't have prepared responses. You want to control space and develop with purpose rather than entering well-known theoretical battles.

Black chooses a solid, French-like setup with e6, preparing to control d5 and stake a claim in the center without committing to e5. This move keeps options flexible—Black can follow up with d5, c5, or even a kingside fianchetto depending on our setup. It's a classical response that avoids giving us any immediate tactical wins.

Our bishop finds its natural home on b2, where it controls the long diagonal (a1-h8) and puts gentle pressure on Black's center and kingside. This is the hallmark of the Polish Opening: we're building influence along the long diagonal while keeping pieces on modest squares. The bishop is well-placed here and doesn't block our own pieces.

Black develops with Nf6, preparing to support the center and keep options open for either a kingside fianchetto or classical development. This is flexible development—the knight doesn't commit to a specific plan yet but can reroute depending on where we go. It's the active move in this position.

We push forward on the queenside to prevent Black's thematic ...a6 expansion and gain space. This aggressive lunge isn't just about territory; it restricts Black's pieces and forces them to make concrete decisions about the a-file. Now Black faces a choice: advance with ...a6 to secure space, or allow us to potentially plant a pawn on b6.

Black stops our pawn and prevents b6 from becoming a outpost, showing practical restraint even though it uses a move on the flank. By playing ...a6, Black gains the a-file and prepares counterplay on the queenside while keeping the center flexible. This is a solid prophylactic move that prevents our expansion but locks in our pawn structure.
